task.title: Reuse existing tasks for progressive refinement
task.summary: Change roadmap task creation semantics so related user requests refine existing active tasks instead of spawning duplicate new tasks by default.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki treats user requests as iterative refinements of existing roadmap intent when a related active task already exists.
acceptance: Roadmap documentation states progressive refinement as the default: search active tasks/sprints before creating new work.
codewiki_task create can identify related active tasks without an explicit task id using overlapping spec paths, code paths, labels, and intent text.
When a related active task is found, creation reuses and additively refines that task instead of creating a duplicate.
Refinement preserves existing task identity and merges new spec/code paths, labels, research ids, acceptance, non-goals, verification, and useful delta details without clobbering existing content.
Tool guidance tells agents to refine existing tasks/sprints/docs before creating new tasks.
Tests cover automatic reuse/refinement and non-related task creation.
non_goals: No LLM embedding service or external semantic search.
No mutation of closed/cancelled tasks unless an explicit task id is supplied.
No full project-management duplicate detection UI.

evidence: Added build smoke coverage for creating an initial task, refining it through a related create request, verifying merged fields and priority escalation, and creating unrelated work separately.
Existing smoke coverage confirms a duplicate create is reused and a broad-scope but different-intent create is not reused solely due to spec overlap.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added deterministic related-task matcher and refinement merge helpers to application/roadmap.ts.
Task creation results now report refined tasks and mark create action changed when refinement occurs.
Pi tool guidance now communicates progressive refinement to agents.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Added build smoke coverage for creating an initial task, refining it through a related create request, verifying merged fields and priority escalation, and creating unrelated work separately.
Existing smoke coverage confirms a duplicate create is reused and a broad-scope but different-intent create is not reused solely due to spec overlap.
code_change_evidence: Added deterministic related-task matcher and refinement merge helpers to application/roadmap.ts.
Task creation results now report refined tasks and mark create action changed when refinement occurs.
Pi tool guidance now communicates progressive refinement to agents.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Roadmap documentation states progressive refinement as the default: search active tasks/sprints before creating new work.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: .codewiki/kb/system/roadmap.md now documents progressive refinement and the create-vs-refine decision.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: codewiki_task create can identify related active tasks without an explicit task id using overlapping spec paths, code paths, labels, and intent text.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: appendRoadmapTasks now calls findRelatedRoadmapTask for create inputs without an explicit id; matching scores active tasks using spec/code/research path overlap, labels, title relation, and intent token similarity.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: When a related active task is found, creation reuses and additively refines that task instead of creating a duplicate.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: appendRoadmapTasks now calls refineRoadmapTaskFromInput for explicit or related matches and returns reused/refined results instead of allocating a new task id.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Refinement preserves existing task identity and merges new spec/code paths, labels, research ids, acceptance, non-goals, verification, and useful delta details without clobbering existing content.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: refineRoadmapTaskFromInput merges arrays with unique(), escalates priority only upward, fills empty summary/outcome, appends differing outcome/delta text as refinement detail, and leaves title/status identity intact.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tool guidance tells agents to refine existing tasks/sprints/docs before creating new tasks.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: extensions/codewiki/src/adapters/pi/index.ts promptGuidelines now instruct agents to check active tasks/sprints and prefer refinement; system adapter/API/compiler docs also encode the rule.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tests cover automatic reuse/refinement and non-related task creation.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/builds.test.mjs covers create->related create reuse/refine and unrelated create new; scripts/smoke-test.mjs existing broad-scope duplicate/distinct coverage passes with the new matcher gate.
npx tsc --noEmit passed; npm test passed.

closure_brief.user_intent: Make CodeWiki progressively refine existing active tasks and docs when user requests relate to current work instead of creating a new task every time.
implemented_changes: Documented progressive refinement in roadmap, compiler, API, and adapter specs.
Added deterministic active task matching for codewiki_task create.
Added additive refinement merging for paths, labels, research ids, acceptance, non-goals, verification, outcomes, and delta details.
Updated task creation result reporting for refined tasks.
Updated Pi tool guidance for agents.
Added smoke tests for reuse/refinement and unrelated task creation.
acceptance_evidence: All acceptance criteria mapped to docs/code/tests evidence.
non_goals_preserved: No LLM embedding service or external semantic search.
No mutation of closed/cancelled tasks unless explicit id is supplied.
remaining_risks: Heuristic task matching may need tuning from real usage.
Sprint record refinement beyond active task matching remains future work.

risks: Heuristic matching can still over- or under-match edge cases; current gate avoids broad path-only reuse and excludes closed/cancelled tasks for implicit matching.
Active sprint scope refinement is documented but this implementation refines matching active tasks, not sprint records directly.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.
